text:
This work discusses the architecture and performance of image registration implemented in the recently released Insight ToolKit version 4 (ITK4). ITK4 is releasing a large software base that is designed to achieve higher levels of repeatability in computation and also help to set new standards of open source scientific computing. The ITK4 unified transform framework provides a model for expressing both low dimensional (affine) and high dimensional (deformation field) transformations. Additionally, this work includes multivariate image support that was missing in previous versions, composite transforms for complex composed transformations, as well as multi-threaded measurement and optimization classes to take advantage of multi-core processing resources.

ITK4 focuses a significant amount of effort on reducing the effort required to the user to parameterize the optimizer. Estimating the parameter scales using automated heuristics leads to more consistent steps sizes for very different types of transforms such as 3D rotations and 3D translations. In this work, we demonstrate the flexibility of the proposed framework by applying it to a number of applications including building templates, brain segmentation in the presence of pathologies, and asymmetry analysis. In order to evaluate the benefits of the proposed framework, we carry out a comparative study using a pediatric brain labeling task and show that segmentation accuracy obtained using the new implementation of ITK4 is higher than those obtained using an older implementation in ITK3. The ITK4 registration suite offers a complete and flexible environment ready to support and facilitate future clinical research and complex neuroinformatics applications.
